channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Production Optimization: Inference Cost and Performance Control
item.description: 1. Introduction: The Dual Pain Points of Inference Cost and Performance in Customer Service This is Part 7 of the series 8 Weeks from Zero to One: Full-Stack Engineering Practice for a...

item.title: Generate ER Diagram from SELECT Queries (JOIN Analysis Tool)
item.description: No installation required. Runs entirely in your browser. Introduction When working with existing systems, have you ever struggled with complex SQL like this? Too many JOINs to understand the...

item.title: Stop Writing AI Agent Prompts Like It's 2023: The Framework That Makes Your OpenClaw Agent Actually Work
item.description: Your agent isn't broken. Your SOUL.md is. I've deployed dozens of AI agents. WhatsApp bots, Telegram assistants, Discord helpers — you name it. And for months, I kept hitting the same wall: The...

item.title: What is Griductive and how the puzzles are made
item.description: Griductive is a daily deductive logic puzzle game where you play as a detective and determine who is innocent and who is a suspect using pure logic. Some people on the grid may or may not carry a...

item.title: Infinite+ Free Developer Tools – No Signup, No Tracking
item.description: I built a collection of 90+ online tools that run entirely in your browser. No accounts, no data collection – just tools. JSON / XML / CSV ↔ JSON converters Base64, JWT, URL, HTML entity...

item.title: Cognitive Prosthesis: How to Turn AI from a Tool into a Co-Author of Complex Systems
item.description: Why do some people get superficial answers from AI, while others get architectural solutions, production-ready code, and deep analysis? We analyzed 116 user dialogues with AI and discovered a...

item.title: Deno 2.0 in 2026: The Node.js Alternative That Finally Got It Right
item.description: Deno 2.0 in 2026: The Node.js Alternative That Finally Got It Right When Ryan Dahl (the creator of Node.js) announced Deno in 2018, he listed 10 things he regretted about Node. Deno was his...

item.title: ASSIGNMENT 20
item.description: Search in a Rotated Sorted Array (O(log n) Binary Search) Searching in a rotated array might look tricky at first — but with the right approach, it becomes elegant and efficient 🔥 Let’s break it...

item.title: System Design for Beginners: How to Design a URL Shortener in 2026
item.description: System Design for Beginners: How to Design a URL Shortener in 2026 System design interviews terrify most developers. The questions are open-ended, the scope is massive, and there's no single...

item.title: 10 AI Code Review Tools That Actually Caught Bugs My Team Missed
item.description: I planted 23 bugs across a real codebase. Here's what each tool found and what slipped through. Let me tell you how this started. Three months ago, a bug made it to production that had survived...

item.title: AI influencer awards season is upon us
item.description: First came the AI beauty pageant. Then the AI music contests. Now, there is an award for AI Personality of the Year - perhaps the inevitable next step for the AI influencer economy as it...

item.title: Topical Dancer is propulsive, playful, and political
item.description: Last week's recommendation, Sotomayor's Wabi Sabi, has a very particular vibe that you don't find in a lot of records. One of the few things it called to mind was 2022's Topical Dancer from...

item.title: Do you want to build a robot snowman?
item.description: On the latest episode of the Equity podcast, we recapped CEO Jensen Huang’s GTC keynote and debated what it means for Nvidia’s future.

item.title: Cursor admits its new coding model was built on top of Moonshot AI’s Kimi
item.description: Building on top of a Chinese model feels particularly fraught right now.

item.title: Crimson Desert dev apologizes for use of AI art
item.description: Reviews of Crimson Desert have been mixed, but the bigger issue for the game has been the discovery of what appeared to be AI-generated assets in the final release. Now the developer has...

item.title: The best deals you can already grab from Amazon’s Big Spring Sale
item.description: Amazon’s Prime Day sale may arrive earlier than usual this year, but there’s a deal event happening much sooner: Amazon’s annual Big Spring Sale, which is happening March 25th through the 31st....

item.title: Elon Musk unveils chip manufacturing plans for SpaceX and Tesla
item.description: Elon Musk recently outlined ambitious plans for a chip-building collaboration Tesla and SpaceX — but he has a history of overpromising.

item.title: Delve accused of misleading customers with ‘fake compliance’
item.description: An anonymous Substack post accuses compliance startup Delve of “falsely” convincing “hundreds of customers they were compliant” with privacy and security regulations.

item.title: Musk says he’s building Terafab chip plant in Austin, Texas
item.description: Elon Musk announced plans to build a Terafab plant in Austin, Texas, that will be jointly run by Tesla and SpaceX. The goal is to eventually build chips at scale for robotics, artificial...

item.title: The secret story of the vocoder, the military tech that changed music forever
item.description: The vocoder was never supposed to be a revolution in music. It wasn't supposed to be anything in music, really. Its development began a century ago, when an engineer at Bell Labs was looking for a...

item.title: The pint-sized Sonos Roam 2 is more than 20 percent off this weekend
item.description: Sonos is no stranger to the occasional sale. The iconic audio company routinely runs sales around Black Friday, Amazon Prime Day, and the Super Bowl, though it’s rare to see substantial discounts...

item.title: An exclusive tour of Amazon’s Trainium lab, the chip that’s won over Anthropic, OpenAI, even Apple
item.description: Shortly after Amazon announced its $50 billion investment in OpenAI, AWS invited me on a private tour of the chip lab at the heart of the deal.

item.title: AI was everywhere at gaming’s big developer conference — except the games
item.description: AI was everywhere at the GDC Festival of Gaming this year. Vendors at the event pitched generative AI tools for things like making AI-driven NPCs and even entire games from a chat box. On the show...

item.title: Online age checks came first — a VPN crackdown could be next
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more news about online age verification and your privacy, follow Emma Roth. The Stepback...

item.title: Are AI tokens the new signing bonus or just a cost of doing business?
item.description: Maybe tokens really will become the fourth pillar of engineering compensation. But engineers might want to hold the line before embracing this as a straightforward win.

item.title: Halide co-founder is suing former partner for bringing source code to Apple
item.description: Lux Optics co-founder Sebastiaan de With made headlines when he joined Apple in late January. The company was behind Halide, one of the most popular photography apps for the iPhone, which gained a...

item.title: Publisher pulls horror novel ‘Shy Girl’ over AI concerns
item.description: Hachette Book Group said it will not be publishing “Shy Girl” over concerns that artificial intelligence was used to generate the text.

item.title: Why Wall Street wasn’t won over by Nvidia’s big conference
item.description: Despite investor fears of an AI bubble, Nvidia's latest conference shows that most in the industry aren't concerned by that possibility.

item.title: FBI Warns Russian Hackers Target Signal, WhatsApp in Mass Phishing Attacks
item.description: Threat actors affiliated with Russian Intelligence Services are conducting phishing campaigns to compromise commercial messaging applications (CMAs) like WhatsApp and Signal to seize control of...

item.title: I Tried DoorDash’s Tasks App and Saw the Bleak Future of AI Gig Work
item.description: I recorded videos of myself doing laundry, scrambling eggs, and walking around the park in DoorDash’s new Tasks app, where gig workers are paid to train AI.
